intro.en: PNG is one of the most common image formats, but sometimes you need a different format — smaller file size, better browser support, or specific use cases. All PNG converters below run in your browser with no server upload.

question.en: What is the best format to convert PNG to?

answer.en: For web use, convert PNG to WebP or AVIF for smaller file sizes with similar quality. For photos, JPG is a good choice. For icons and illustrations, keep SVG if available, or use PNG itself.

question.en: Does converting PNG to JPG lose quality?

answer.en: Yes — JPG uses lossy compression, so some quality is lost. You can control the quality level in our converter (85% is a good balance). PNG to WebP also supports lossy compression but often achieves better results than JPG at the same file size.

question.en: Can I convert PNG to WebP for free?

answer.en: Yes, our PNG to WebP converter is completely free and runs in your browser. It supports batch conversion and lets you control output quality.
